meta.purpose: QA corrections for code routing, QSO-25-07-NH status, CT discharge/inspection resources, infection-control routing, and handout gaps

code_routing_corrections.reason: The CT LTCOP Discharge Portal is a staff-facing hub for involuntary nursing facility transfer and discharge procedures, portal access, and supporting documents. It should route to C-series discharge/transfer support rather than generic D-series rights resources.
code_routing_corrections.human_review_note: D-series rights codes may still appear as secondary issues, but this discharge portal resource should trigger first on C-series discharge/transfer complaints.

code_routing_corrections.reason: The LTCOP Involuntary Discharge Portal User Manual is a procedural guide for involuntary discharge notices and should attach to C-series discharge/transfer complaints, not general D-series rights complaints.
code_routing_corrections.human_review_note: Use for CT electronic discharge notice workflow support; avoid showing it automatically for all D-series rights complaints.

code_routing_corrections.reason: The SNF Transfer and Discharges / CMS QSO-25-14-NH portal update deck is specific to SNF transfer and discharge guidance and CT portal updates.
code_routing_corrections.human_review_note: Keep as a staff-facing reference for C-series discharge complaints.

code_routing_corrections.reason: The Consumer Voice SNF transfer/discharge notice handout explains federal transfer/discharge notice rights and should be routed consistently with other discharge resources.
code_routing_corrections.human_review_note: Ensure Batch 4 and Batch 7 discharge handouts use consistent C-series routing.

code_routing_corrections.reason: The CT DPH Nursing Home Inspections process page is a general survey/inspection process explainer and is not tied to a single NORS complaint code.
code_routing_corrections.human_review_note: Trigger by topic, such as inspection_process, instead of suggesting it is specific to A/B complaint codes.

code_routing_corrections.reason: The CT eLicense inspection portal is a broad facility-lookup tool for survey/complaint reports and plans of correction, so narrow A/B code routing may mislead.
code_routing_corrections.human_review_note: Trigger by topics such as survey_results or inspection_reports so staff can use it across complaint types.

code_routing_corrections.reason: Consumer Voice's infectious disease outbreak visiting handout is primarily an infection/outbreak resource. NORS F13 is the infection-control complaint code.
code_routing_corrections.human_review_note: G-series may be secondary if the facts involve group rights or retaliation during an outbreak, but primary routing should be F13.

code_routing_corrections.reason: The Consumer Voice family advocacy COVID-19 fact sheet is most closely aligned with infection control and outbreak complaint support.
code_routing_corrections.human_review_note: Show as F13 support with COVID-context caveat.

code_routing_corrections.reason: CDC's infection-prevention page is written for residents about infection prevention and expectations in long-term care settings, matching F13 infection control.
code_routing_corrections.human_review_note: Route as a high-authority infection-control resource.

code_routing_corrections.reason: The Consumer Voice/Justice in Aging issue brief on unnecessary drugs and antipsychotic medications supports medication/psychotropic complaint review rather than G-series routing.
code_routing_corrections.human_review_note: Use as an ombudsman-staff resource for F12 medication complaints, especially psychotropic/antipsychotic concerns.

code_routing_corrections.reason: WG-005 already instructs that K and L complaints are outside-agency/system complaints and should not receive Appendix PP F-tags.
code_routing_corrections.human_review_note: Downstream crosswalk components should honor WG-005 and avoid auto-attaching F-tags to K/L complaints.

resource_corrections.current_value: CT LTCOP How to Obtain Important Discharge Documents
resource_corrections.corrected_value: CT Long Term Care Ombudsman Program - Official Program Page
resource_corrections.reason: RR-024 should remain a general CT LTCOP program page now that the dedicated CT discharge portal is confirmed separately.
The generic CT LTCOP home page should not be coded as a discharge-specific resource once RR-B7-001 is available.
The CT LTCOP discharge portal is a state program resource, distinct from primary statute or regulation.
The resource was produced in a COVID-19 context and needs a clear caveat while still being generally useful for outbreaks.
The resource has COVID-19 framing, so topics should include covid_context for display caveats.

source_verification_rows.source_name: CMS QSO-25-07-NH Revised Long-Term Care Surveyor Guidance
source_verification_rows.preferred_use: Historical context only for medication surveyor guidance changes before QSO-25-14-NH revised effective dates.
source_verification_rows.limitations: The direct QSO-25-07-NH path is unstable and an expired version should not be treated as current authority.

source_verification_rows.source_name: CMS QSO-25-14-NH Revised LTC Surveyor Guidance
source_verification_rows.preferred_use: Primary CMS memo reference for the current effective date of QSO-25-07-NH-related guidance and SNF transfer/discharge updates.
source_verification_rows.limitations: Check latest CMS postings for future revisions.

source_verification_rows.preferred_use: Primary CT-specific portal for involuntary discharge/transfer resources and supporting documents.
source_verification_rows.limitations: Linked PDFs may need periodic currency checks after statutory or CMS updates.

source_verification_rows.source_name: CT DPH FLIS Blast Fax Page
source_verification_rows.preferred_use: Official CT DPH page for facility blast fax notices and regulatory communications.
source_verification_rows.limitations: Individual notices were not enumerated in this pass.

source_verification_rows.source_name: CT DPH eLicense Nursing Home Inspection Documents Portal
source_verification_rows.preferred_use: Primary CT-specific portal for individual nursing home inspection and complaint investigation reports and plans of correction.
source_verification_rows.limitations: Search requires date-range entry and URL stability should be monitored.

source_verification_rows.preferred_use: Primary authority for NORS code definitions and routing rules.
source_verification_rows.limitations: Does not itself provide regulatory citations.

resource_currency_rows.title: LTCOP Involuntary Discharge Portal User Manual PDF
resource_currency_rows.display_caveat: User manual is dated December 2022 and may not fully reflect later CT public acts or CMS guidance.

resource_currency_rows.title: SNF Transfer and Discharges CMS QSO-25-14-NH LTCOP Portal Updates PDF
resource_currency_rows.display_caveat: Treat as current only if it aligns with the live QSO-25-14-NH memo and latest CT discharge statutes.

resource_currency_rows.title: Consumer Voice Tips for Facility Visits During an Infectious Disease Outbreak
resource_currency_rows.display_caveat: Produced in a COVID-19 context; generally applicable to infectious disease outbreaks but policy examples should be interpreted with current CDC/CMS guidance.

resource_currency_rows.title: Consumer Voice Family Advocacy for Nursing Home Residents During COVID-19
resource_currency_rows.display_caveat: COVID-19 framed resource; use as advocacy/training support and pair with current CDC guidance.

resource_currency_rows.title: CDC Infection Prevention and Long-Term Care Facility Residents
resource_currency_rows.display_caveat: High-authority CDC resource; verify update date if used after 2026.

resource_currency_rows.title: CT DSS Requesting a Hearing - Nursing Home Transfer/Discharge Appeals
resource_currency_rows.display_caveat: Use as primary CT-specific source for nursing home discharge appeal timelines; verify procedural updates annually.

resource_currency_rows.title: CT General Statutes 19a-535a Residential Care Homes Transfer/Discharge Appeal
resource_currency_rows.display_caveat: MRC/residential care home timelines differ from nursing facility timelines and must not be conflated.

workflow_update_rows.guidance: For Connecticut involuntary transfer/discharge complaints, confirm whether the facility is a nursing home under CGS 19a-535 or a residential care home/MRC under 19a-535a; use the CT LTCOP Discharge Portal as the state-specific hub; rely on CT DSS and CGS sources for appeal timelines; treat training PDFs and handouts as support resources, not primary authority.
workflow_update_rows.do_not_say: Do not tell residents they simply have 30 days to decide on an appeal without explaining the shorter stay deadlines. Do not imply training materials or handouts are primary legal authority.

workflow_update_rows.guidance: For F13 infection control complaints, pair high-authority CDC infection prevention content with Consumer Voice outbreak handouts, clearly identifying Consumer Voice pieces as COVID-context advocacy/training resources.
workflow_update_rows.do_not_say: Do not present Consumer Voice outbreak handouts as CMS or CDC guidance.

workflow_update_rows.guidance: For K-series outside-agency and L-series non-facility complaints, no Appendix PP F-tags should be assigned. Use NORS Table 2 and 45 CFR 1324.19(a)(4) to route to appropriate outside agencies and avoid implying facility violations.
workflow_update_rows.do_not_say: Do not attach F-tags to K or L series complaints or describe them as nursing facility violations.

tooltip_rows.label: QSO-25-07-NH Status
tooltip_rows.short_help: QSO-25-07-NH content is confirmed but the CMS PDF URL is unstable.
tooltip_rows.details: The original QSO-25-07-NH PDF path is unstable and an expired version may exist. Treat QSO-25-14-NH as the operative CMS source for effective dates and use advocacy issue briefs only as training resources until a stable CMS PDF is confirmed.

tooltip_rows.label: Infection Handouts Caveat
tooltip_rows.short_help: Consumer Voice infection handouts are COVID-era advocacy tools.
tooltip_rows.details: Consumer Voice infection outbreak handouts are advocacy/training resources, not CMS or CDC authority. Pair them with CDC infection-prevention content and flag their COVID-19 context.

tooltip_rows.label: Discharge Appeal Timelines - CT
tooltip_rows.short_help: Deadlines differ for nursing homes and MRCs in Connecticut.
tooltip_rows.details: For nursing homes under CGS 19a-535, residents have 60 days from the notice date to request a hearing, but must request within 20 days to stay the discharge. For residential care homes/MRCs under 19a-535a, residents have 10 days to request a hearing.

human_review_flags.issue: No confirmed resident-facing medication error handout from acceptable sources.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Do not add a medication-error handout unless a live resident-facing source from CMS, CT agencies, NORC, or Consumer Voice is confirmed.

human_review_flags.issue: Infection-control handouts have COVID-specific framing that may not fully align with future outbreak policies.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Display a COVID-context caveat for RR-B7-010 and RR-B7-011 and cross-check CDC guidance for non-COVID outbreaks.

human_review_flags.issue: QSO-25-07-NH CMS PDF URL remains unstable or unavailable.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Use QSO-25-14-NH as the operative memo for effective date and related discharge guidance; use Consumer Voice/Justice in Aging issue briefs only as training references until a stable CMS QSO-25-07-NH PDF is confirmed.

human_review_flags.issue: CT LTCOP Discharge Portal supporting PDFs may not fully reflect the most recent CT public acts and CMS discharge guidance.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Ask CT LTCOP whether newer versions of the discharge portal user manual and QSO-25-14-NH portal training exist.
